meta_title: For Freelancers: SaaS starter kit to Solve no project ideas (Intermediate Checklist)
meta_description: Learn how to SaaS starter kit and overcome no project ideas. A Intermediate-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Freelancers: SaaS starter kit to Solve no project ideas
content.seo_description: Stop staring at a blank screen. Use this intermediate SaaS starter kit checklist with Antigravity and Claude Code to ship profitable projects faster.
content.answer_block: The 'no project ideas' trap is solved by modularizing your development pipeline. By leveraging a robust SaaS starter kit, you transform boilerplate into a repeatable asset. Use Antigravity to scaffold architecture and Claude Code to automate implementation, turning vague concepts into deployable, high-value client applications in mere hours.
content.intro:
You sit before a glowing cursor, the silence of a blank IDE amplifying the pressure of a looming deadline. The 'no project ideas' paralysis isn't a lack of creativity; it’s a lack of leverage. When you treat every project like a fresh start, you’re not building a business—you’re reinventing the wheel while your billable hours evaporate.

True mastery lies in the architecture of the 'Starter Kit.' By standardizing your auth, database, and UI patterns, you stop coding features and start shipping solutions. With the heavy lifting handled by Antigravity and Claude Code, you shift from a reactive freelancer to a proactive AI-driven architect, ready to dominate the market with speed and precision.

steps.title: Define Your Core Architecture
steps.description: Stop reinventing the stack. Establish a base boilerplate that handles authentication, billing, and database connectivity. This creates the foundation for every future project.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a high-performance SaaS starter template using Next.js 15, Tailwind CSS, and Supabase. Include pre-configured Clerk auth and Stripe subscription logic.

steps.title: Automate Database Schema Design
steps.description: Use AI to normalize your data models based on your project requirements, ensuring scalability from day one.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a Prisma schema for a multi-tenant SaaS application. Include tables for users, organizations, subscriptions, and logs with proper indexing.
steps.claude_code_command: claude edit --file schema.prisma --instruction 'add project-specific models for task management'

steps.title: Inject Business Logic Modules
steps.description: Instead of writing boilerplate, use Claude Code to generate domain-specific logic, such as report generation or AI-driven workflows.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a modular server-side action to handle PDF report generation using OpenAI's API and Puppeteer.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'implement server action for report generation'

steps.title: Implement Rapid UI Components
steps.description: Utilize existing design systems to ensure your product looks professional without manual CSS styling.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a reusable dashboard layout component using Shadcn UI that supports light/dark mode and responsive sidebar navigation.
steps.claude_code_command: claude edit --file components/layout.tsx --instruction 'add responsive dashboard sidebar'

steps.title: Automate Deployment Pipelines
steps.description: Ensure your project is CI/CD ready by configuring automated testing and deployment to Vercel or Railway.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a GitHub Actions workflow file that runs ESLint, type-checking, and unit tests on every pull request.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'configure github actions for automated deployment'

pro_tips: Always store your 'business logic' in a separate package or folder to ensure it can be ported between different starter kits.
Use Claude Code to write integration tests for your auth flow before adding a single feature; it saves hours of debugging later.
Treat your starter kit like a product—version it and document the API surface so you don't forget how your own boilerplate works.
Implement feature flags early so you can toggle experimental AI features without breaking the core subscription logic.
Pre-bake your environment variables management using a strictly typed system like T3 Env to prevent runtime configuration errors.
content.next_steps: Once your starter kit is finalized, deploy a 'v0' version to a dummy domain and use it as a lead magnet to show potential clients your development speed.
content.conversion_block: Stop building from scratch and start scaling your freelance business with a repeatable, high-speed workflow. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
